_meta.keyKnowledge: We rehearse and perform devised and scripted drama for different audiences in various settings.

_source.w: We rehearse and perform devised and scripted drama for different audiences in various settings.
y: Performing for different audiences teaches us to adapt our communication for impact.
Rehearsal discipline helps us bring our best creative work to the stage.
Engaging with audience responses deepens our understanding of how drama communicates.
eg: We ask 'How did the performers use the elements of drama effectively?' when watching others.
We rehearse our drama to refine timing, expression and character development.
We present our work in both formal settings (stage) and informal settings (classroom).
We consider how different audiences might respond to our drama.
We use peer feedback to improve our performance before presenting to a wider audience.
We evaluate the effectiveness of design elements like sound and costume in our performance.
